steps.title: First account deposits
steps.content: Depositing $x$ dollars once per month for 12 months contributes $12x$ dollars annually.

steps.title: Second account deposits
steps.content: Depositing $y$ dollars twice per month for 12 months means $2 \times 12 = 24$ deposits, contributing $24y$ dollars annually.

steps.title: Set up the total equation
steps.content:
The total across both accounts is \$5,400:
$$5400 = 12x + 24y$$

steps.title: Match to the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — $5,400=12x+12y$: This would mean $y$ is deposited only once per month, not twice. ✗
  • Choice B — $5,400=12x+24y$: $12x$ for once-monthly $x$ deposits, $24y$ for twice-monthly $y$ deposits. ✓
  • Choice C — $5,400=24x+12y$: This reverses the deposit frequencies — $x$ would be twice-monthly and $y$ once-monthly. ✗
  • Choice D — $5,400=24x+24y$: This would mean both accounts receive deposits twice per month. ✗
That confirms B is correct.
